---
title: "Evidence"
subtitle: "The runnable scoreboard. Every claim in these docs resolves to a scenario here."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: false
---

This is the one place the docs compare `neuroim` against raw
`nibabel`+`numpy` — because here the comparison is a *measurement*,
not framing. Each row is a scenario under
[`examples/scenarios/`](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/tree/main/examples/scenarios):
a single neuroimaging task implemented twice, with a `pytest` file
asserting the verdict.

The rules: every public feature has a scenario; every pain point is
filed before it is fixed; every verdict below is regenerated from the
scenario `REPORT.md` files at render time, so this table cannot drift
from the source of truth.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import sys
from pathlib import Path

cwd = Path.cwd()
for cand in (cwd, *cwd.parents):
    inc = cand / "_includes" / "evidence_scan.py"
    if inc.exists():
        sys.path.insert(0, str(inc.parent))
        break
from evidence_scan import find_repo, scenario_rows

repo = find_repo(cwd)
rows = scenario_rows(repo)
gh = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios"

print("| # | Scenario | Verdict | Report |")
print("|---|----------|---------|--------|")
for num, slug, title, verdict in rows:
    print(f"| {num} | {title} | {verdict} | [REPORT]({gh}/{slug}/REPORT.md) |")
print(f"\n*{len(rows)} scenarios. Regenerated from `examples/scenarios/*/REPORT.md` at render time.*")
```

## How to read a verdict

A scenario is a **win** if the `neuroim` form improves on at least one
of *read-time*, *safety*, or *inspectability* and ties or improves on
the rest. Surfaced pain points are filed as `PAIN-N` in the scenario's
`REPORT.md` even when the scenario wins overall — they are how the
suite stays honest. A verdict that says it *falsifies* a mission claim
is not a failure of the docs; it is the suite catching the library and
is tracked to a closing fix.

## Run them

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:tests:. python -m pytest examples/scenarios -q
```

The shared fixture is
`tests/fixtures/realistic_bold.py::make_realistic_bold()` — a
deterministic 32×32×24×40 BOLD. Two tiny fixtures
(`golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_*.nii.gz`) ship for the doc snippets and
exercise the same code paths.

## See also

- [Spec: contract-failure vocabulary](../spec/contract-failure-vocabulary.html) — the refusals these scenarios assert.
- [Concepts: Contract failures](../concepts/contract-failures.qmd) — the narrative behind the refusals.
- [Start](../start/01-image.qmd) — the on-ramp whose every "Refuses to" block links back into this table.
